articles.title: Xcel gas rate increase in Minnesota to be lower than expected
articles.summary: Xcel Energy customers in Minnesota are likely to see a natural gas rate increase lower than initially expected, reduced from 8.2% to 4.1% for residential customers under a proposed settlement. Since customers have already been paying a temporary 6.8% increase, they could receive a refund with interest if the Minnesota Public Utilities Commission approves the settlement this fall. Xcel serves over 430,000 natural gas customers across the state.

Xcel serves over 430,000 natural gas customers across the state.

articles.title: FedEx (NYSE: FDX) removes 1.300% Notes due 2031 from NYSE listing
articles.summary: FedEx Corp. has filed a Form 25 with the SEC to delist its 1.300% Notes due 2031 (CUSIP 31428XBX3) from the New York Stock Exchange. Both the NYSE and FedEx confirmed compliance with the voluntary withdrawal procedures under 17 CFR 240.12d2-2. The filing indicates a "neutral" impact and sentiment, without disclosing whether the notes will trade over-the-counter or remain registered after delisting.
articles.content: FedEx Corp. has filed a Form 25 with the SEC to delist its 1.300% Notes due 2031 (CUSIP 31428XBX3) from the New York Stock Exchange. The filing indicates a "neutral" impact and sentiment, without disclosing whether the notes will trade over-the-counter or remain registered after delisting.

articles.title: Union Pacific Corp Stock (UNP) Moved Down by 3.89% on May 28: Facts Behind the Movement
articles.summary: Union Pacific Corp (UNP) stock fell by 3.89% on May 28, primarily due to the Surface Transportation Board pausing its review of the proposed merger with Norfolk Southern, citing a need for more information and an extended environmental review. This regulatory uncertainty, coupled with broader macroeconomic concerns about accelerating inflation impacting interest rate expectations, led to increased market skepticism despite Union Pacific's strong Q1 2026 financial performance. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus for UNP, but regulatory hurdles and potential overvaluation pose risks.
articles.content: Union Pacific Corp (UNP) stock fell by 3.89% on May 28, primarily due to the Surface Transportation Board pausing its review of the proposed merger with Norfolk Southern, citing a need for more information and an extended environmental review. articles.title: Union Pacific slides as U.S. rail regulator pauses UP–Norfolk Southern merger review pending more information
articles.summary: Union Pacific's stock dropped by 4.2% after the U.S. Surface Transportation Board (STB) paused the review of its proposed merger with Norfolk Southern. The STB requested additional information due to unclear or underdeveloped portions of the revised application, putting the environmental review on hold and likely extending the timeline for approval. This regulatory friction is seen by investors as an added hurdle, contributing to the stock's decline.

articles.title: Georgia PSC approves plan to lower overall rates, deliver annual savings of $50 per year for Georgia Power residential customers – Company Announcement - FT.com
articles.summary: The Georgia Public Service Commission (PSC) has approved a plan to lower overall rates for Georgia Power customers, resulting in annual savings of approximately $50 for typical residential customers. This decision follows a stipulated agreement between Georgia Power and the PSC's Public Interest Advocacy Staff, stemming from cases related to fuel cost recovery and storm cost recovery after Hurricane Helene in 2024. The rate decrease is effective next month and is expected to deliver total annual savings of $285 million for all Georgia Power customers.
articles.content: The Georgia Public Service Commission (PSC) has approved a plan to lower overall rates for Georgia Power customers, resulting in annual savings of approximately $50 for typical residential customers. This decision follows a stipulated agreement between Georgia Power and the PSC's Public Interest Advocacy Staff, stemming from cases related to fuel cost recovery and storm cost recovery after Hurricane Helene in 2024. The rate decrease is effective next month and is expected to deliver total annual savings of $285 million for all Georgia Power customers.
